input.command:
ADMIN=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
# try connecting without auth to see state, and check which is primary
for i in 0 1 2; do
echo "=== pod $i ==="
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-$i -- mongosh --quiet -u admin -p "$ADMIN" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "print('isMaster:', db.hello().isWritablePrimary); print('me:', db.hello().me)" 2>&1 | head -5
done

input.command:
A=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
echo -n "$A" | xxd | tail -3
echo "len: ${#A}"
# Try authenticationDatabase as the password db variations and SCRAM-SHA-1
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin:$A@localhost:27017/admin?authSource=admin" --eval "print('primary', db.hello().isWritablePrimary)" 2>&1 | head -3

input.command:
for pair in "app:app-user-password:appdb" "appuser:app-user-password:appdb" "app-user:app-user-password:appdb" "readonly:readonly-user-password:appdb" "admin:admin-user-password:test"; do
 u=${pair%%:*}; rest=${pair#*:}; sec=${rest%%:*}; adb=${rest##*:}
 P=$(kubectl get secret $sec -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
 r=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u "$u" -p "$P" --authenticationDatabase "$adb" --eval "print('OK')" 2>&1 | tail -1)
 echo "$u @ $adb => $r"
done

input.command:
A=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
for u in adminuser admin_user adminUser dbadmin superuser mongoadmin clusterAdmin myUserAdmin userAdmin; do
 r=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u "$u" -p "$A" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "print('OK')" 2>&1 | tail -1)
 [ "$r" = "OK" ] && echo ">>> FOUND: $u" || echo "$u: fail"
done

input.command:
A=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}'|base64 -d)
CANDS="admin root administrator superadmin mongoadmin dbadmin adminuser admin_user adminUser sysadmin master owner manager operator karma benchmark mongo test user1 globalAdmin"
for u in $CANDS; do
 kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u "$u" -p "$A" --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "1" >/dev/null 2>&1
done
echo "probes sent"
# Now inspect log: lines with AuthenticationFailed that are NOT UserNotFound => user exists
kubectl logs -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 --since=20s 2>&1 | grep -i "Authentication failed" | grep -v "UserNotFound" | tail -20
echo "=== existing users (non UserNotFound) DONE ==="
input.description: Probe candidate admin usernames and detect existing ones via logs
